commonIssues:
Telangana recently migrated its DL data from the state-run 'Anywhere Anywhere' system to the national 'Sarathi' system. Many older licenses did not sync correctly, showing as 'No Record Found'.

How to fix: Visit your local RTO and apply for 'Record Synchronization' or 'Backlog Entry'. You must provide your old license details for the data to be manually pushed into the national Sarathi database.

RTOs in Hyderabad (e.g., Kondapur, Uppal) use advanced automated tracks. Applicants often fail for stopping the vehicle midway or not completing the '8' shape within the time limit.

How to fix: Practice the '8' shape and 'H' shape tracks in a training ground. Do not use your phone or look back while reversing—use the mirrors. Ensure your vehicle does not stall, as a single engine cutoff can lead to failure.

Persistent shortages of smart cards in Telangana have led to months of delay in issuing the physical license.

How to fix: Download the 'T-App Folio' or mParivahan app. The Telangana Police are officially integrated with these apps and will accept the digital version of your DL during road-side inspections.

Difficulties in renewing a license issued in another state that has expired, as a 'No Objection Certificate' (NOC) is required from the original RTO.

How to fix: First, apply for an NOC from the parent RTO via the Sarathi portal. Once the NOC is issued digitally, you can apply for 'Change of Address' and 'Renewal' at your current Telangana RTO.
